name: Signals
description: Universal inbound event stream — every meaningful event from any source (CRM webhooks, ad platforms, web analytics, third-party iPaaS, custom integrations) lands here as a typed signal that scripts can query, score, and act on.

properties.description: Stable unique identifier for this signal — typically <provider>:<source_event_id> to allow idempotent re-delivery.
Inbound source that delivered this signal. Used for routing, debugging, and provider-level filtering.
Concrete origin within the provider. Examples: 'google-ads', 'meta-ads', 'ga4', 'hubspot.contact.created', 'stripe.invoice.paid'.
Normalized signal class. Scripts filter on this rather than provider-specific source strings.
Importance signal scoring scripts use to prioritize follow-up. Default 'low' unless mapping declares otherwise.
Timestamp the underlying event happened in the source system (not when Personize received it).
Timestamp Personize ingested this signal. Used for backfill detection and freshness scoring.
Email used to resolve this signal to a Contact. May be empty if anonymous; resolution will be attempted on later signals.
Company domain used to resolve this signal to a Company when no email is available.
Native CRM record ID when the source provides one. Allows direct-link resolution without email/domain matching.
Whether this signal was successfully linked to a Contact or Company. 'pending' signals are retried on later signals; 'no-match' signals stay quarantined for review.
Personize Contacts record ID this signal resolved to, if any.
Personize Companies record ID this signal resolved to, if any.
Short human-readable description. Examples: 'Visited /pricing', 'Replied to outreach email', 'New funding round announced'.
AI-generated one-paragraph explanation of what happened and why it matters. Filled by extraction or research scripts.
Raw provider payload as JSON string. Kept for re-extraction and debugging. Scripts should not parse this directly — use mapped properties instead.
Channel the signal originated on, when applicable.
Campaign or sequence the signal is associated with, when applicable.
Suggested change to the entity's AI score from this signal alone. Aggregated by lead-quality and account-score scripts.
Whether this signal requires human or agent follow-up. Set by analyze.* and score.* scripts during processing.
Names of scripts/operations that have already consumed this signal. Prevents duplicate processing across cycles.
Free-form tags scripts can use to filter or group. Examples: 'high-intent', 'enterprise', 'churn-risk'.
